response.justification: Sarah Smith has extensive backend and distributed-systems expertise, demonstrated through her work on event-driven platforms (e.g., GISCollective) and real-time applications (e.g., Dunnhumby media). However, her background lacks explicit experience with multi-agent AI systems in production, fintech infrastructure (e.g., credit risk, payment flows), or regulatory-compliant environments, which are core requirements for this role. Her recent work in AI-agent infrastructure (MCP, agentic workflows) at re:cinq is promising but not yet sufficient to meet the 10+ years of senior leadership required.
strengths: Strong backend and distributed-systems expertise with event-driven architectures
Experience scaling engineering teams and improving development processes (e.g., CI/CD, TDD)
Recent engagement with AI-agent frameworks (MCP, agentic workflows) at re:cinq
concerns: No demonstrated experience with multi-agent AI systems in production or fintech infrastructure
No clear track record in regulated or high-stakes commercial environments (e.g., credit risk, compliance)
Limited exposure to fintech-specific domains like algorithmic working-capital management or real-time credit decisioning
